file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16- AMENDMENT #2 TO THE DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.changed_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a." creates an omission where the territory is undefined. This now directly contradicts Section 1 which states "the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States and [*]"
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms of this Amendment, the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States and [*].
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the distributor's exclusivity based on territory and marketing activities. The changed text removes the territory and marketing activity requirements, broadening the distributor's exclusivity without defining its scope. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines the territory as "the United States and [*]". By removing the specific territory constraint in the changed text, it creates an inconsistency regarding the geographical limits of the distributor's exclusivity, as the agreement now defines two different territories depending on which clause is being read. The removal also changes a conditional statement into a blanket statement, triggering the contradiction.
